expectations.text: Branches the hotfix from `uat` (NOT directly from `main`) so the fix passes through stakeholder acceptance
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly says 'Branch from main' and the 'Why branch from main, not develop?' section justifies branching from main. Skips uat acceptance entirely.

expectations.text: PR sequence is hotfix to uat, then uat to main (not hotfix directly to main)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 says 'Open hotfix/null-payload-fix -> main directly. This is the difference vs. a normal feature: hotfixes go straight to main'.

expectations.text: Source-ref check on uat accepts hotfix/* as a valid source
expectations.evidence: Source-ref check on uat is not mentioned at all; the flow bypasses uat in the PR sequence.

expectations.text: After the hotfix reaches main, instructs forward-merge from uat back to develop to keep them in sync
expectations.evidence: Step 5 back-merges main into uat and develop, but the framing is wrong (forward-merge should be from uat to develop after uat received the hotfix; here uat itself is being patched after-the-fact). The skill's specific 'forward-merge uat -> develop' pattern is not followed.

expectations.text: Explicitly explains why the forward-merge matters
expectations.evidence: Step 5 says 'If you skip this step, develop and uat still have the bug. The next normal release cycle (develop -> uat -> main) will re-introduce the null pointer'.
No mention of a regression-only exception conditioned on the bug existing only on main; the output uses main-branching as the default rather than as an exception.

expectations.text: CI gates on the hotfix PRs are the same as a normal promotion at each tier (no skipping gates because of urgency)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 lists full gate requirements and says 'Don't shortcut this; the urgency makes mistakes more likely, not less'.

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
